message.content: Sure, I'd be happy to explain unit tests and their usefulness. Unit tests are a type of automated test that verifies the correctness of individual units, or the smallest testable parts, of code. These tests are typically written by developers to ensure that each component of a program functions as intended. Unit tests are useful because they help catch bugs early in the development process, improve code quality, and make refactoring and changes to the codebase safer. They also serve as documentation for the code, helping new developers understand how a particular part of the system is supposed to work, and can significantly reduce the time required for testing.
